description.content: Submit a document for extraction

formdata.description: (Required) The document to extract. The maximum file size is 100MB.

(Required) The output document format. Kensho Extract offers three document types: `hierarchical`, `hierarchical_v2`, and `general`.
Please refer to our [overview page](home) for a detailed breakdown of which model will provide the most optimal output for your use case.

(Required) Identifies whether the document is scanned or is a native pdf. This must be `true` or `false`.
See the [OCR](ocr) page for more information.
(Required) Use our newest model to extract data from complex tables more accurately than ever.                     This must be `true` or `false`.

Use our newest model to extract data from figures.                     This must be `true` or `false`.
See the [Figure Extraction](figex) page for more information.
Whether to return the locations of images. This must be `true` or `false`.
Whether to return the relations between items. This must be `true` or `false`.
A custom document identifier.
The priority level. Anything besides `low` will be considered `high` priority. (This can only be one of low)
This specifies the pages to extract. Page numbers start at 1. Format: `1-5,7,11,14-16`.

Whether to return absolute PDF page numbers in the output. Absolute PDF page numbers refer to the original page numbers requested, while relative page numbers start at 0 and increment by 1 for each page requested

This can be `structured_document`, `structured_document_with_locations`, or `structured_document_with_char_offsets`. It determines if the response format will include location bounding box information.
Including this parameter requires making a call to `/v3/extractions/download-url/{request_id}` to obtain a URL for downloading the document from AWS.

response.name: The request was successfully created.

response.name: This can be any combination of the required parameters are not provided, a parameter that is invalid, or if a specific pages is requested in the `pages` parameter and that page is not in the document. E.g., requesting page 57 for a 10 page document.

item.name: Upload URL To Submit A Document For Extraction.

request.name: Upload URL To Submit A Document For Extraction.

description.content: Creates the request and returns a pre-signed upload URL to upload the document for extraction.

formdata.description: (Required) This can be `structured_document`, `structured_document_with_locations`, or `structured_document_with_char_offsets`. It determines if the extracted document format will include location bounding box information and character offsets.

(Required) The output document format. Kensho Extract offers three document types: `hierarchical`, `hierarchical_v2`, and `general`.
Please refer to our [overview page](home) for a detailed breakdown of which model will provide the most optimal output for your use case.

(Required) Identifies whether the document is scanned or is a native pdf. This must be `true` or `false`.
See the [OCR](ocr) page for more information.
(Required) Use our newest model to extract data from complex tables more accurately than ever. This must be `true` or `false`.

Use our newest model to extract data from figures. This must be `true` or `false`.
See the [Figure Extraction](figex) page for more information.
A custom document identifier.
The priority level. Anything besides `low` will be considered `high` priority. (This can only be one of low)
This specifies the pages to extract. Page numbers start at 1. Format: `1-5,7,11,14-16`.
This specifies the total number of pages to extract.

response.name: The request was successfully created.

item.name: Mark The Upload As Complete To Start Extraction

request.name: Mark The Upload As Complete To Start Extraction

description.content: Call this after the document has been uploaded to the pre-signed URL returned by `/v3/extractions/upload-url`. The extraction process will not begin until this endpoint is called.

formdata.description: (Required) The request_id for the extraction.

response.name: The request was marked as uploaded successfully.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Kensho Extract allows users to quickly transform their unstructured documents into a machine-readable format that identifies titles, subtitles, paragraphs, tables, and footers detected within the document in their natural reading order. Kensho Extract interprets messy page layout, structuring text into cohesive paragraphs that can be effectively analyzed and searched.

The Kensho Extract API V3 has incorporated changes to how users must call the API. Please note there are more required fields in API V3 than API V2 (deprecated). The following fields are *mandatory* for `/v3/extractions`: file, document_type, ocr and enhanced_table_extraction.

API V3 introduces new upload and download functionality, allowing the upload of the original document and retrieval of the extracted document output via pre-signed URLs. The pre-signed URLs expire after 15 minutes.
These new endpoints must be called in the following order. - `/v3/extractions/upload-url` - followed by POST'ing the document to the `url` provided in the response - `/v3/extractions/upload-complete` - `/v3/extractions/download-url/{request_id}` - followed by a calling the GET `output_url` provided in the response
